data.text: The code path itself is fine, but the repo-wide test suite is blocked by external VCS-stamping failures in unrelated packages, so I can’t honestly claim the acceptance gate is green. I’m writing the required no-changes rationale now so the run has explicit evidence of the blocker instead of an empty exit.

No commit was made.

I verified the federation wiring is already present in production:
- `EnableHubMode` loads persisted state via [`cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go:51-58`](\/tmp\/ddx-land-wt-3520433475\/cli\/internal\/server\/federation_hub.go#L51)
- `persistFederationLocked` saves state via [`cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go:336-343`](\/tmp\/ddx-land-wt-3520433475\/cli\/internal\/server\/federation_hub.go#L336)

I also captured the required evidence:
- [`/.ddx/executions/20260507T013329-adb3aeb4/decisions.md`](\/tmp\/ddx-land-wt-3520433475\/.ddx\/executions\/20260507T013329-adb3aeb4\/decisions.md)
- [`/.ddx/executions/20260507T013329-adb3aeb4/no_changes_rationale.txt`](\/tmp\/ddx-land-wt-3520433475\/.ddx\/executions\/20260507T013329-adb3aeb4\/no_changes_rationale.txt)

Blocker: `cd cli && go test ./...` is not green here because unrelated packages fail with `error obtaining VCS status: exit status 128` during `go build`/lint-driven tests, notably `internal/agent/workerprobe` and `tools/lint/runtimelint`. The federation package itself passes, and `deadcode` reports zero remaining dead symbols in `internal/federation`.
